# RizlDizl

> A small macOS menu bar app that drives per-key RGB on Razer keyboards natively — lighting your keys as you type with reactive effects. No Razer Synapse, no cloud, no account. A native Mac alternative for Razer keyboard lighting.

RizlDizl reverse-engineers the Razer lighting protocol so it can paint per-key RGB directly from a macOS menu bar app. It reacts as you type (ripples, crawlers, letters), separates a static background from a layered effect, and talks only to the keyboard on your desk. Runs on macOS 13+ (Apple Silicon and Intel). The app and all backgrounds are free; the effects are a paid upgrade.

## What it does
- Per-key reactive RGB on Razer keyboards, native on macOS — no Razer Synapse required.
- Separate a free static background from a layered effect (Ripple, Centipede, Alpha Stick, Alpha Splat).
- Lives in the menu bar — no window, no dock icon.

## Keyboard compatibility
- Verified: Razer Ornata Chroma V2.
- Should work (same Ornata-family protocol): Ornata Chroma, Ornata, Cynosa V2, Huntsman V2, BlackWidow V3.

## Privacy
- Lighting a key as you press it requires macOS Accessibility and Input Monitoring permission.
- The keystroke drives one frame of animation and is thrown away — nothing is logged, saved, or sent anywhere; there is no server.
- The keystroke-handling code is open source: https://github.com/ScienceIsNeato/RizlDizlScaryBitz

## Pricing
- App and backgrounds: free.
- Effects: $9.99/year, with a 30-second in-app preview of every effect before you pay.

## Get it
- Home and download: https://www.rizldizl.com/
